description: Cache timer hooks — tracks response timestamps and active session state for TTL display

hooks.command: TS=$(date +%s); echo $TS > $HOME/.claude/.last-claude-response; SID=$(cat | jq -r '.session_id // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$SID" ]; then mkdir -p $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer && echo $TS > $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer/$SID; fi; find $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer -type f -mtime +1 -delete 2>/dev/null; true

TS=$(date +%s); echo $TS > $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer-active; SID=$(cat | jq -r '.session_id // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$SID" ]; then mkdir -p $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer && echo $TS > $HOME/.claude/.cache-timer/${SID}.active; fi; _R="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"; [ -z "$_R" ] && _R="$HOME/CascadeProjects/claude-cache-timer"; bash "$_R/scripts/cache-expiry-warn.sh"
